observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management shall:

a. Issue a demand letter to notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances.
b. Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to AO and personnel who still have unliquidated cash advances;
c. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports on time to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and, 
d. Take appropriate action for the full settlement of the year-end balance of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AOs are currently providing the supporting documents to liquidate the previous cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the inventory committee to immediately submit Physical Inventory Report to the COA audit team for review and validation since physical inventory taking has been completed as of date. Further, reconcile the results of the inventory taking with the books of accounts and effect adjustment(s), if any.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the aforementioned unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents.

We recommended that Management require the Disbursing Officer to submit to the accounting section the supporting documents. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension will be issued. 

Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer thoroughly check/review the completeness of its supporting documents prior to the granting of cash advances to prevent any suspension and/or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and the aforementioned COA Circulars in the preparation, recording of financial reports/documents, and the prompt submission of the said financial reports and transaction documents in compliance with the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the aforementioned unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents to the audit team on or before February 28, 2023. Else, such transactions will be suspended and/or disallowed in an audit.

Thereafter, submit the financial reports and disbursement vouchers completely based on the monthly check disbursement journal to the audit team to prevent issuance of Notice of Suspensions and/or Disallowances.

We recommended that Management thru the inventory committee to immediately submit Physical Inventory Report to the COA audit team for review and validation since physical inventory taking has been completed as of date.

Further, reconcile the results of the inventory taking with the books of accounts and effect adjustment(s), if any.

We recommended that Management require the Disbursing Officer to submit to the accounting section the supporting documents of the unexpended/unspent balance of ₱614,000.00 from his cash advances or return/refund the same to the treasurer’s office through the issuance of official receipt, and submit to the COA auditor on or before March 15, 2023. Otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued. 

Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to thoroughly check/review the completeness of liquidation reports and its supporting documents prior to submission to the Office of the Auditor to prevent any suspension and/or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort and install strategies to fast tract settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following remedial measures:

a) Payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled;

Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and    disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries; and 

Stop the practice of granting additional cash advance when previous cash advances remained unliquidated/unsettled.
We recommended that Management should follow the existing rules and regulation on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as stated in COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that management send demand letters to barangays with past due unliquidated fund transfer and require immediate liquidation, Likewise, management should strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and ensure that funds release for the implementation of the projects are duly accounted for.

We recommended that Management shall:
 
Discontinue the procurement of gasoline/fuel through reimbursement and petty cash fund. 

Direct the personnel in charge in the procurement of gasoline to prepare and submit to the Auditor the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption on official travel in compliance with COA Circular No. 71-61 dated September 26, 1977.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers for the payment of gasoline expenses are duly supported with all the duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets to avoid suspension in audit.

Ensure that prescribed documentary requirements were attached before claims/payment for gasoline procurement are processed, approved and paid.

Strictly observe the proper procedures and appropriate mode of procurement in the purchase of gasoline for all offices of the LGU   as prescribed in Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials, drugs and medicines as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-General Services Officer or other officer/s in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items to (i) ensure timely submission of copies of Purchase Orders to the Audit Team within five working days from issuance thereof pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 09, 2009; (ii) notify the Audit Team on the scheduled acceptance of all deliveries; and (iii) submit copies of pertinent delivery documents and reports within the prescribed period to facilitate timely inspection and other related audit procedures and in order that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We require that prescribed procedures in keeping and recording of purchases under Sections 118, 119 and 120 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume I be followed; and 

 Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to accept all purchases of supplies and property by using the prescribed form of IAR. Record immediately the supplies or property in the property records on the basis of IAR and other documents. Maintain Stocks Cards and Property Cards. Forward the IAR and other supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant to record the deliveries in the appropriate ledger cards.
We require that provisions stated in Republic Act No. 3846 be followed to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for assistance from the Municipal Engineering Office in the conduct of Pre-Inspection on the damaged properties and Post-Inspection of the work accomplished and also in the preparation of Pre and Post-Inspection Reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also, prepare the Report of Waste Materials and Property Index Card summarizing all the repairs and maintenance performed on each service vehicle and submit the same together with the Pre and Post Inspection Report COA for evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to, henceforth, include the required information on the appropriation, utilization and the balance of unexpended DRRMF balances and other information regarding the inventories and PPE as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002 and 2014-002; and all officials concerned to submit the following reports:
Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances 

Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) 

Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that management:
 
Require the Municipal Budget Officer not to allow the payments of transactions    without appropriations. Limit disbursement of funds to what has been duly appropriated in the annual budget to avoid incurrence of overdraft.

Observe strict compliance with the provision of Section 336 of RA 7160.

Require the officials/employees concerned to refund the amount of P2,580,508.00 which was incurred without an appropriation and payment was not authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan and Department of Budget and Management.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

Record the purchases of fuel in bulk under an inventory account and not as direct expense. Fuel consumption should be properly controlled and accounted for through approved Requisition and Issue Voucher or its equivalent.

Henceforth, stop the practice of purchasing thru reimbursement basis.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant recognize and transfer the Local Road Networks from the Public Infrastructure Registry to the books of accounts.  Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to revisit the map of the municipality’s local road networks and determine the proper cost for each local road through review of records.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management shall:
Issue a demand letter to notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances.
Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to AO and personnel who still have unliquidated cash advances;
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports on time to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and,
Take appropriate action for the full settlement of the year-end balance of cash advances.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management thru the inventory committee to immediately submit Physical Inventory Report to the COA audit team for review and validation since physical inventory taking has been completed as of date.
Further, reconcile the results of the inventory taking with the books of accounts and effect adjustment(s), if any.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management refrain from incurring and charging expenses not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities, otherwise such will be disallowed in audit.
We further recommended that Management should comply strictly with the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.   Personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO shall be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU.  The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.  30 percent of the 5% LDRRMF should be for Disaster Response and the 70 percent allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer comply with the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as required under COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management prepare MOA signed by both parties prior to the release of check to the recipient-barangays for succeeding fund transfer.
We further recommended that Management require the aforementioned recipient-barangays to liquidate the funds; any unused funds will be refund/return to the Municipal Treasurer prior to the approval of the Liquidation Report. Henceforth, observe COA Circular Nos.2012-001 on the documentation and recording requirements on fund transfers.
Likewise, stop the granting of fund transfers to barangays who failed to submit accomplishment and liquidation reports.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and all those responsible in the control and issuance of fuel to submit the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents on the abovementioned fuel expenditure. Otherwise, the same will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for Auditor’s review and evaluation.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the aforementioned unsubmitted disbursement vouchers together with their complete supporting documents.

We recommended that Management shall:
Strictly require both the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer to properly reconcile subsidiary records and monthly payroll deduction with the GSIS monthly computation and/or statement of account;
Immediately stop the current practice of deliberate exclusion of the GSIS loans amortization of the concerned officials and employees from the payroll. Hence, both the Accounting and HR Departments to enforce the deduction of all amortizations for loans (consolidated, policy, and emergency loans, among others) from the member’s salary; and,
Refund the GSIS loans amortization/repayment of the concerned officials and employees totaling ₱1,382,110 which have been shouldered by the Municipality due to non-deduction and/or exclusion from the monthly payroll. Therefore, disallowed in the audit.
Submit justification and/or explanation of why this deficiency occurred.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Accountant endeavor to revisit its policy of allowing Dr. Gay B. Ardanas, Department Head of RHU Talisayan to have five (5) active globe lines and stop the excessive ones.
We also recommended that Management should adhere to the regulation requiring checks drawn to be made payable directly to the creditor, stop the practice of paying through reimbursement basis to officials and employees, and ensure the withholding of the required amount of taxes under existing regulations.
Submit; a) Certification by the head of the agency or his/her representatives that all National Direct Dial (NDD), National Operator Assisted Calls and International Operator Assisted Calls are official calls, b) authority from the head of the agency for entitlement to Mobile phone allowance and c) certification from the Chief Accountant and Property Officer that the employee was not issued a postpaid plan for a mobile phone.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point Person to furnish the COA audit team a copy of the corresponding Annual GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
